reasoning: Analysis of the FRED series AAA10Y shows that the spread between Moody's Aaa corporate bonds and the 10-year Treasury yield is currently at 1.16 (as of November 7, 2025), which represents a local peak compared to the previous week (1.05). Historical data and trend analysis (fetch_ts_fred) indicate that the series is currently above its short-term linear trend line (1.129), suggesting a higher likelihood of mean reversion or stabilization rather than further widening in the near term. The statistical tool estimates a 34% probability of the value being higher than 1.16 by November 16. In the medium term (December 2025 to February 2026), while the overall trend for the year 2025 has been slightly upward from the very low spreads seen in late 2024 (approx 0.7 to 0.8), the market environment of potential Federal Reserve easing (as indicated in market outlooks) typically exerts downward pressure on credit spreads as economic confidence stabilizes. Therefore, probabilities remain below 50% across all horizons, trending toward the 0.5 neutral point as the forecast horizon and uncertainty increase.
